metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5359
metadata.comment:
Rationale: "In vitro ubiquitination assays using purified proteins showed that TRAF6, in conjunction with Ubc13/Uev1A, effectively delivered the ubiquitin moieties to PSD-95, which did not occur when TRAF6, UbK63, Ubc13, Uev1A, or PSD-95 was omitted from the reaction mix (Fig. 3E)."

"Interactions between endogenous PSD-95 and TRAF6, Ubc13, Uev1A, or CYLD in mouse forebrain were also detected (Fig. S3 F-I)."

"The activity-dependent assembly/disassembly of K63-polyUb chains may involve dynamic regulation of the E2-E3-substrate-DUB multiprotein complex we have identified in the PSD. The Ubc13/Uev1A dimer remains the only known K63-selective E2 that, together with the RING-domain family E3 ligase TRAF6, catalyzes the assembly of K63-polyUb chains on substrates. Our results suggest that the Ubc13/Uev1A-TRAF6 complex binds the PSD-95 NT and delivers ubiquitin moieties to cognate lysine sites.". Experimental description: M&M: "high-density (1 × 106) hippocampal neurons were prepared from E18 rat embryos"
